title: polypodium subconfluens
intro: Polypodium subconfluens, also known as the Maidenhair Fern, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family and is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Polypodium subconfluens include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a valuable treatment for various ailments. Polypodium subconfluens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for internal use, and its dried leaves can be used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
